titleEn: From novelty to necessity: Why tulip growers are betting on robotic disease selection
titleZh: 从新奇到必需：郁金香种植者为何押注机器人病害筛选
summaryZh: 本文报道了荷兰H2L公司推出的郁金香病害筛选机器人（H2L Selector 180/225），该机器人可识别并处理花床中的病株，已售出139台，用户评分4.1/5。尽管机器人正从试验走向主流，但种植者强调，其大规模普及仍需在可靠性和性能上进一步改进。文章通过四位用户的反馈，展示了机器人从“新奇”到“必需”的转变趋势，为智慧种植领域的自动化病害防控提供了实践参考。
featuredReason: 本文展示了机器人病害筛选在花卉种植中的成功商业化案例，其从试验到主流的路径、用户反馈及对可靠性的要求，对智慧畜牧行业（如畜禽健康巡检、病畜识别）的自动化技术落地具有重要借鉴意义。
contentFull: From novelty to necessity: Why tulip growers are betting on robotic disease selection 16-06 | Smart farming | ArticleLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintThe H2L Selector 180 and its larger brother, the 225, are robots that recognise and treat diseased tulips in the beds. The robots have working widths of 180 and 225 cm respectively and are produced exclusively for tulip growing. H2L has now sold 139 of them.The H2L Selector has moved beyond the experimental stage. With 139 machines sold and users giving it an average score of 4.1 out of 5, robotic disease selection is becoming mainstream in tulip production. Yet growers stress that widespread adoption will depend on further improvements in reliability and performance. Future Farming asked 4 users of the H2L Selector 180 about their experiences with the robot. The conclusion: with an average score of 4.1 on a scale of 5 they are generally positiveLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint Lucas van de Weijer Future Farming robot expertMore aboutBrandstof (Energie)GewasbeschermingPrecisielandbouwrobotsadLEARN MORERelated articles22-06 | NewsNew retrofit spot spraying system for every type of sprayer09-04 | NewsBankrupt robot manufacturer Agrointelli sold in parts24-11-2025 | NewsNew Holland presents R4 robot for orchardsPremium23-09-2025 | ArticleAutonomous potato selection robot PotatoSelector300 ready for practice Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
H2L Selector 180及其更大的兄弟机型225，是能够识别并处理花床中患病郁金香的机器人。这两款机器人的作业宽度分别为180厘米和225厘米，专为郁金香种植生产。H2L目前已售出139台。

H2L Selector已走出试验阶段。凭借139台的销量和用户给出的平均4.1分（满分5分）评价，机器人病害筛选正在郁金香生产中成为主流。然而，种植者强调，大规模普及仍有赖于可靠性和性能的进一步提升。Future Farming采访了四位H2L Selector 180的用户，了解他们对这款机器人的使用体验。结论是：在5分制的评分中，他们平均给出了4.1分，总体持积极态度。
author: Lucas van de Weijer
contentHtml: The H2L Selector has moved beyond the experimental stage. With 139 machines sold and users giving it an average score of 4.1 out of 5, robotic disease selection is becoming mainstream in tulip production. Yet growers stress that widespread adoption will depend on further improvements in reliability and performance.
